\begin{table}[htbp]
\centering
\caption{Table 1: Summary statistics for the three Fama--French factors}
\label{tab:factor_summary}
\small
\begin{threeparttable}
\begin{tabular}{lrrrrrrrr}
\toprule
Factor & $N$ & Mean (\%) & Std (\%) & $t$(Mean) & Sharpe & AC(1) & Ann.\ Mean (\%) & Ann.\ Vol (\%) \\
\midrule
MKT\_RF & 402 & 0.674 & 10.627 & 1.337 & 0.063 & -0.002 & 8.089 & 36.813 \\
SMB & 402 & 0.860$^{***}$ & 5.416 & 3.121 & 0.159 & 0.008 & 10.323 & 18.763 \\
HML & 402 & 0.417 & 5.018 & 1.644 & 0.083 & -0.069 & 5.010 & 17.383 \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\begin{tablenotes}[flushleft]
\footnotesize
\item MKT\textunderscore RF is the value-weighted market excess return. SMB (small minus big) and HML (high minus low) are the Fama--French size and value factors constructed from Chinese A-share stocks.
\item Newey--West (1987) $t$-statistics with 12 lags.
\item $^{*}p<0.10$, $^{**}p<0.05$, $^{***}p<0.01$.
\end{tablenotes}
\end{threeparttable}
\end{table}

\begin{table}[htbp]
\centering
\caption{Table 2: Pairwise correlations among the three factors}
\label{tab:factor_corr}
\small
\begin{threeparttable}
\begin{tabular}{lrrr}
\toprule
Factor & MKT-RF & SMB & HML \\
\midrule
MKT\_RF & 1.000 & 0.332 & -0.235 \\
SMB & 0.332 & 1.000 & -0.224 \\
HML & -0.235 & -0.224 & 1.000 \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\begin{tablenotes}[flushleft]
\footnotesize
\item Pearson correlations computed over the full sample period.
\end{tablenotes}
\end{threeparttable}
\end{table}

\begin{table}[htbp]
\centering
\caption{Table 3: Summary statistics for the six $2\times3$ base portfolios used to construct SMB and HML}
\label{tab:six_portfolios}
\small
\begin{threeparttable}
\begin{tabular}{lrrrrr}
\toprule
Portfolio & $N$ & Avg Ret (\%) & Std (\%) & $t$(Mean) & Avg \#Stocks \\
\midrule
SL & 402 & 1.487$^{**}$ & 13.385 & 2.414 & 227.751 \\
SM & 402 & 1.838$^{***}$ & 12.299 & 3.109 & 334.199 \\
SH & 402 & 1.963$^{***}$ & 12.249 & 3.176 & 204.085 \\
BL & 402 & 0.759 & 10.953 & 1.435 & 231.112 \\
BM & 402 & 0.830 & 9.769 & 1.589 & 281.361 \\
BH & 402 & 1.119$^{*}$ & 10.056 & 1.948 & 258.530 \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\begin{tablenotes}[flushleft]
\footnotesize
\item Portfolios are the six $2\times3$ intersections of size (S = small, B = big) and book-to-market (L = low, M = medium, H = high).
\item Newey--West (1987) $t$-statistics with 12 lags.
\item $^{*}p<0.10$, $^{**}p<0.05$, $^{***}p<0.01$.
\end{tablenotes}
\end{threeparttable}
\end{table}

\begin{table}[htbp]
\centering
\caption{Table 4: Average monthly excess returns (\%) for $5\times5$ size--book-to-market portfolios}
\label{tab:25port_excess_returns}
\small
\begin{threeparttable}
\begin{tabular}{lrrrrr}
\toprule
Size $\backslash$ BM & BM1 & BM2 & BM3 & BM4 & BM5 \\
\midrule
S1 & 1.929 & 1.763 & 2.010 & 2.591 & 2.224 \\
S2 & 0.917 & 1.593 & 1.287 & 1.535 & 1.636 \\
S3 & 0.807 & 0.837 & 1.140 & 1.203 & 1.302 \\
S4 & 0.692 & 0.701 & 0.948 & 1.094 & 0.977 \\
S5 & 0.474 & 0.735 & 0.571 & 0.725 & 0.795 \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\begin{tablenotes}[flushleft]
\footnotesize
\item Portfolios are the 25 intersections of five size quintiles and five book-to-market quintiles, both sorted independently each June.
\item S1 = smallest size; BM1 = lowest book-to-market.
\item Reported values are time-series averages of monthly excess returns (\%).
\end{tablenotes}
\end{threeparttable}
\end{table}

\begin{tablenotes}[flushleft]
\footnotesize
\item Time-series regressions: $R_{it} - R_{ft} = \alpha_i + b_i(R_{mt}-R_{ft}) + s_i\,\mathrm{SMB}_t + h_i\,\mathrm{HML}_t + \varepsilon_{it}$.
\item Portfolios are the 25 $5\times5$ size--BM intersections. S1 = smallest size; B1 = lowest book-to-market.
\item Newey--West (1987) $t$-statistics with 12 lags in parentheses.
\item $^{*}p<0.10$, $^{**}p<0.05$, $^{***}p<0.01$.
\end{tablenotes}
\end{threeparttable}
\end{table}

\begin{table}[htbp]
\centering
\caption{Table 6: Alpha diagnostics for the FF3 model across 25 test portfolios}
\label{tab:alpha_diagnostics}
\small
\begin{threeparttable}
\begin{tabular}{lr}
\toprule
Metric & Value \\
\midrule
n\_portfolios & 25.000 \\
mean\_alpha\_pct & 0.117 \\
mean\_abs\_alpha\_pct & 0.211 \\
max\_abs\_alpha\_pct & 0.920 \\
n\_sig\_10pct & 5.000 \\
n\_sig\_5pct & 4.000 \\
n\_sig\_1pct & 2.000 \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\begin{tablenotes}[flushleft]
\footnotesize
\item Diagnostics are computed across the 25 size--BM test portfolios.
\item RMSE and MAE are in percentage points per month.
\end{tablenotes}
\end{threeparttable}
\end{table}

\centering

\label{tab:pricing_errors}
\small
\begin{threeparttable}
\begin{tabular}{lrrr}
\toprule
Portfolio & Actual (\%) & Fitted (\%) & Error (\%) \\

S4B3 & 0.948 & 0.938 & 0.010 \\

\midrule

S5B2 & 0.735 & 0.248 & 0.488 \\
S5B3 & 0.571 & 0.362 & 0.209 \\

\midrule
Summary &  &  &  \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\begin{tablenotes}[flushleft]
\footnotesize
\item Actual = time-series average monthly excess return.
\item Fitted = FF3 model-predicted average excess return.
\item Error = Actual $-$ Fitted.
\item Portfolios are grouped by size quintile; horizontal rules separate quintiles.
\item Cross-portfolio RMSE = 0.306\%, MAE = 0.223\%.
\end{tablenotes}
\end{threeparttable}
\end{table}
